meta.title: South America + China Trip Plan
meta.publicSafetyNote: No booking references, passport details, private addresses, or contact details should be stored in this public repo.
assumptions: Steve returns to the United States on 2026-07-05 from Hong Kong.
China itinerary details after city/date level are pending Steve's afternoon update.
Packing advice is based on late-June and early-July seasonal conditions and should be refined with near-term forecasts before departure.

route.notes: Land at PVG in the afternoon. Keep evening light because this follows GRU-ADD-PVG long-haul travel.
Meetings currently proposed for morning and early afternoon China time.
Likely hot, humid, and rainy. Keep one fast-dry outfit ready.
Warmer and potentially drier than the Yangtze Delta, but still summer heat.
Leave one nicer outfit available for meetings or dinners.
Hot, very humid, and likely thunderstorm risk. Prioritize breathable layers and waterproof carry.
Similar to Shenzhen/Hong Kong: hot, humid, showers possible.
Hot and humid. Keep passport, onward ticket, and cross-border essentials easy to reach.
Buffer day before U.S. return. Do laundry or repack here if needed.
Return-to-U.S. day. Keep U.S. entry documents and one clean flight outfit in carry-on.

weatherAssumptions.expected: Hot and humid, with rain risk around the plum-rain season.
weatherAssumptions.packingImpact: Use breathable shirts, fast-dry pants, compact umbrella or rain shell, and shoes that can handle wet pavement.

weatherAssumptions.expected: Hot summer weather, usually drier than Shanghai/Hangzhou.
weatherAssumptions.packingImpact: Short sleeves work during the day; keep one smarter layer for indoor AC and meetings.

weatherAssumptions.expected: Very hot and humid, with thunderstorms or typhoon-season rain possible.
weatherAssumptions.packingImpact: Prioritize quick-dry clothes, sandals or waterproof-friendly shoes, compact umbrella, and a dry bag for electronics.

weatherAssumptions.expected: Southern Hemisphere winter: Buenos Aires can be quite cold, Sao Paulo can be cool, and Rio is milder but rainy.
weatherAssumptions.packingImpact: Do not pack only summer clothes. Bring real long sleeves plus a warm layer that also works on long flights.

packingPlan.strategy: Pack for three realities: cold/cool South America nights, rainy Rio/South China, and hot humid China city hopping. Keep documents and electronics in carry-on; use one laundry round instead of overpacking.

items: 2 long-sleeve shirts or thin base layers
1 lightweight button-down or smart casual shirt
1 warm layer: light fleece, hoodie, or packable jacket
1 compact rain shell or small umbrella
2 shorts or very light casual bottoms
baseline.notes: This is the core set. Buenos Aires can be cold, while China will be hot and humid, so mix short sleeves with real long-sleeve warmth.

items: 1 comfortable walking shoe that can handle rain
baseline.notes: Your old 10 laundry balls is more than needed. Bring a few and plan one wash in Shanghai or Hong Kong.

items: Small medicine kit: stomach meds, painkiller, allergy meds, cold meds, bandages
baseline.notes: Use small travel packaging. South America plus China city hopping makes basic meds and sunscreen worth bringing.

items: H-1B / I-797 / U.S. visa or status documents
Wallet, ID, cards, some cash/RMB, keys
Printed or offline flight and hotel itinerary
MacBook Air, charger, and one cable
baseline.notes: Do not check these. The printed visa/entry-doc item from the old list maps here: print any required visa/entry docs in color if applicable.

items: Small gifts if you already know recipients
Foldable tote for carrying things back from China
A few face masks, not a whole pack
Swim shorts if Rio/Hong Kong water time is likely
Private personal kit in discreet travel-size packaging
baseline.notes: Bring only what has a real use case. Gifts and souvenirs are optional unless you already know who they are for.

items: Too many chargers: do not bring two MacBook chargers unless one is backup for a known need
Too many USB-C cables: 2-3 total is enough
Old badge pickup item unless this trip has a specific event badge
baseline.notes: The old list was safe but heavy. Cut duplicated electronics and full-size toiletries first.

optional: Maya souvenirs only if they are already packed and meaningful
Saudi clothes only if there is a specific dinner/photo/social purpose
Extra MacBook charger only if you want one in checked luggage and one in carry-on
One extra empty packing cube for gifts/documents
openQuestions: Any formal meetings, dinners, or events in Shanghai/Beijing/Shenzhen/HK?
Checked luggage size and airline baggage limits for each segment?
Whether Steve needs to bring gifts, product samples, documents, or electronics back to China.
